data.title: China to place six-month limit on rare earth export licenses for U.S. firms - report
data.description:
China will place a six-month limit on rare earth export licenses granted to U.S. automakers and other manufacturers, people familiar with the matter told The Wall Street Journal, which would give Beijing leverage if tensions with the U.S. escalate again.

China is expected to start approving U.S. companies' license applications soon, possibly within a week of President Donald Trump and China's Xi Jinping signing off on the trade framework reached in London this week.

The temporary licenses will largely involve rare earths used in manufacturing electric vehicles, consumer electronics, military equipment, and wind turbines.

Once China approves the applications, the U.S. will relax some restrictions on sales of jet engines and related parts, as well as ethane, to China.

On Wednesday, Trump said the trade deal with China was done, subject to final approval by him and Xi. "Full magnets, and any necessary rare earths, will be supplied, up front, by China," he added.

Rare earths have become a major bargaining chip for China in negotiations, as the country accounts for nearly 70% of global production and more than 90% of processing. Restrictions on rare earths have already impacted the global automotive industry, raising concerns of supply shortages.

data.title: Tutor Perini subsidiary secures $67M contract
data.description:
• Tutor Perini (NYSE:TPC) subsidiary, Lunda Construction Company, has been awarded a contract by the Minnesota Department of Transportation valued at approximately $67.2M for the I-94 and I-394 Interchange Project in Minneapolis.
  • The scope of work includes repairing 34 bridges and ramps along I-394 and I-94 between downtown Minneapolis and Highway 100.
  • Work is expected to begin in July 2025 with substantial completion anticipated in the summer of 2027.
  • The contract value has been added to the company’s backlog in Q2 2025.

data.title: Ivanhoe restarts part of Kakula copper mine in Congo
data.description:
• Ivanhoe Mines on Wednesday announced the restart of underground mining operations on the Western Side of Kakula Mine on June 7.
  • Mining activities on the eastern side of the Kakula Mine are expected to start imminently, with activities solely focused on developing access drives to a new mining area east of the existing mine workings.
  • Underground water levels in Kakula Mine stabilized; dewatering to commence in Q3 2025.
  • Kamoa-Kakula revises 2025 production guidance to between 370,000 and 420,000 tonnes of copper.
  • Smelter start-up expected to commence in September 2025, with first anode anticipated in October 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chime Financial prices IPO at $27, above expected band, raising $864M
data.description:
San Francisco–based financial technology company Chime (CHYM) priced its initial public offering of 32M shares at $27.00 per share, above its previously marketed range of $24.00-$26.00 band.

The offering consists of 25,900,765 shares of Class A common stock to be sold by Chime and ~6.1M shares of Class A common stock to be sold by existing stockholders.

The offering is expected to raise ~$864 million for Chime, based on the $27 per share price.

Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 4.8M shares.

The shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on June 12, 2025, under the ticker symbol “CHYM.”

The offering is expected to close on June 13, 2025.

data.title: Intensify Therapeutics slumps 27%, prices $2M stock offering
Nvidia and Samsung to take minority stakes in robotics startup Skild AI - report
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) will make minority investments in Skild AI, a U.S.-based robotics software startup, as part of a broader push to their emerging robotics segments, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is investing $25 million, while Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) is contributing $10 million to the Series B funding round, which is being led by a $100 million commitment from Japan’s SoftBank Group. This latest funding round values Skild AI at approximately $4.5 billion, teh report said.

Skild AI is a Pittsburgh-based startup known for its advanced software that powers robotic systems, enabling robots to learn and adapt across various industries.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) that is already a key player in AI chips, sees robotics as a major future revenue driver and has previously invested in companies like Figure AI and Serve Robotics. 

Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) owns stakes in Rainbow Robotics and Physical Intelligence, is taking a measured approach, using the Skild deal to keep a tab on innovation and other competitors.

data.title: TotalEnergies partners with Mistral AI to boost AI integration in multi-energy strategy
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) and French firm Mistral AI have formed a partnership aimed at advancing artificial intelligence innovation to support TotalEnergies' multi-energy strategy, with a particular focus on low-carbon energy solutions

Under the terms of the partnership, a joint innovation lab will be set up, staffed by teams from both companies.

Mistral AI will contribute its AI technologies, and TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) will contribute its expertise in the production of energy, particularly renewable, low-carbon energy, in order to test and design advanced digital solutions. 

“This partnership illustrates the positive impact generative AI can have on a sector as strategic as that of TotalEnergies,” said Arthur Mensch, Chief Executive Officer at Mistral AI, which is backed by Microsoft (MSFT).

Source: Press Release

data.title: Insmed prices capital raise of $750 via stock offering
UK's trade deficit widens in April
data.description:
The British economy shrank 0.3% M/M in April 2025, the first decline in six months, and the biggest since October 2024. It follows a 0.2% growth in March and compares with forecasts of a 0.1% decline.

Services output fell by 0.4%, following growth of 0.4% in March 2025, and was the largest contributor to the fall in GDP. 

The UK trade deficit widened sharply to £7.03 billion in April 2025 from £3.70 billion in March. This marked the largest trade gap since June 2022, driven by falling exports and rising imports. Exports dropped by 3.4%, while goods imports grew 1.2%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Calavo Growers stock soars on $32 per share takeover bid
data.description:
Calavo Growers (NASDAQ:CVGW) has announced that it received an unsolicited, non-binding proposal to acquire all outstanding shares at $32.00 per share.

The identity of the party making the offer has not been disclosed.

The proposed deal structure includes a combination of stock from the acquiring party and cash. 

The company's board is currently reviewing the proposal with legal and financial advisors.

Lastly, the company said that it does not intend to comment or update further unless warranted.

The announcement has caused Calavo Growers' stock (NASDAQ:CVGW) to surge 12.6% after-hours on Wednesday.
